notes: Foundational U.S. high-risk R&D agency whose model shaped later frontier-tech funding approaches.
Primary U.S. civil science funder behind AI institutes, research, and workforce programs.
Flagship U.S. AI research funding network spanning institutes nationwide.
NSF announced $140M to establish seven new National AI Research Institutes.
NSF announced another $100M for five AI institutes and a central hub.
Canada allocated $2B to sovereign AI compute strategy.
Canada launched a $300M AI Compute Access Fund for SMEs as part of the broader compute strategy.
EU launched InvestAI to mobilize EUR200B for AI including a EUR20B gigafactory fund.
India approved the IndiaAI Mission with roughly $1.24B and plans for national AI compute capacity.
A core IndiaAI Mission component centered on GPU access and domestic compute availability.
METI and NEDO launched GENIAC to support Japanese generative AI model development and compute access.
UK committed GBP1B to expand sovereign compute capacity through AIRR and related systems.
Singapore launched NAIIP to support 10,000 enterprises over three years under National AI Strategy 2.0.
PIF launched HUMAIN as a state-backed AI operating and investment vehicle across the full stack.
